headline: Traditional Birth Attendants Still Deliver 8% of Babies in Rural Areas
context: Despite efforts to ensure skilled birth attendance, traditional birth attendants remain active in remote communities.

cards.big: 8% of births in rural Malaysia still attended by traditional birth attendants not midwives
cards.sub: Rate rises to 22% in Orang Asli communities and remote Sabah and Sarawak areas

cards.big: Maternal deaths are 6 times higher in TBA-attended births compared to facility deliveries
cards.sub: Complications requiring emergency intervention cannot be managed outside health facilities

cards.big: Nearest birthing facility is over 2 hours travel from 340 identified remote communities
cards.sub: Geographic access barrier makes facility delivery logistically impossible for some mothers

cards.big: Ethiopia trained 38,000 community health workers to provide safe birth referral since 2018
cards.sub: Community-clinical bridge model reduced home birth complications by 42%

cards.big: The solution is not to ban traditional attendants but to bring skilled care to where mothers live
Should Malaysia establish birthing centers in all communities beyond 1 hour from hospital
